evidence: The paper uses ranger random forests ("Random forests were fitted using the ranger R package on R 4.2...using default parameters with exception of number of trees") which employ impurity-based importance. It interprets magnitude by reporting frequency: "Of the 7 variable groups, 3 (energy, formants and signal quality) were used at least 20 times, 1 (fundamental frequency) was used 18 times and the remaining 3 (utterances, voicing, and frication) were used 8 times or less." However, main conclusions rest on model prediction performance ("acoustic cry characteristics are associated with some 2-year developmental and behavioral deficits"), not on feature importance ranking. Permutation likelihood-ratio tests provide partial corroboration that acoustic features improve model fit beyond covariates.
